creator: lantw44@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Kurt Jaeger from comment #3)
The latest stable pygobject release is 3.28.2. Most GNOME projects use odd minor version numbers to indicate unstable releases.

The reason of keeping outdated releases in ports is that our x11/gnome3 is still at 3.18. GNOME ports are usually updated together, so the current problem is that we failed to catch up with upstream. It doesn't mean that we have to wait for GNOME 3.28 upgrade to happen to update pygobject to 3.28 releases. It usually means there is currently no ports requiring pygobject > 3.18 so nobody requests an update for it.
